claimText: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species categorizes species into 9 distinct categories ranging from Not Evaluated to Extinct.
evidenceExcerpt: IUCN Red List divides species into 9 categories (NE, DD, LC, NT, VU, EN, CR, EW, EX).
examUse: Statement-based question on the structure and classification system of the IUCN Red List.

claimText: The IUCN Green Status of Species measures conservation success and species recovery using 8 categories and a Green Score ranging from 0 to 100%.
evidenceExcerpt: Green Status includes 8 categories and a Green Score (0-100%) indicating proximity to full recovery.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing the purpose and metrics of the IUCN Green Status from the Red List.

claimText: The Indian Courser and Indian Roller have been recently uplisted to the Near Threatened category on the IUCN Red List.
evidenceExcerpt: Indian Courser, Indian Roller, and Rufous-tailed Lark uplisted to Near Threatened.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching Indian bird species to their current IUCN conservation status.

claimText: The Long-billed Grasshopper-warbler has been uplisted to the Endangered category on the IUCN Red List.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to their IUCN status.

claimText: The conservation status of the Green Sea Turtle has improved from Endangered to Least Concern on the IUCN Red List.
evidenceExcerpt: Green Sea Turtle improved from Endangered to Least Concern.
examUse: Statement-based question highlighting recent conservation successes and status downgrades.
